examples.title: ER Diagram to Relational Schema Conversion
examples.description: Algorithm to convert ER model to relational database schema
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Convert_ER_to_Relational
BEGIN
 // 1. Strong Entities
 FOR each strong entity E
 CREATE relation R with all simple attributes
 SELECT primary key of E as primary key of R
 END FOR
 
 // 2. Weak Entities
 FOR each weak entity W with owner E
 CREATE relation R with all attributes of W
 INCLUDE primary key of owner E as foreign key
 COMBINE owner key with partial key as primary key
 END FOR
 
 // 3. Binary Relationships
 FOR each relationship R between entities
 IF 1:1 THEN
 ADD primary key of one entity as foreign key to other
 ELSE IF 1:N THEN
 ADD primary key of 'one' side as foreign key to 'many' side
 ELSE IF M:N THEN
 CREATE new relation with foreign keys to both entities
 COMBINE both foreign keys as primary key
 END IF
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how to convert an ER diagram into relational schema. For 1:1 and 1:N relationships, we add foreign key to one of the tables. For M:N relationships, we create a separate junction table.

examples.title: Check Entity Integrity Constraint
examples.description: Algorithm to verify primary key uniqueness
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Check_Primary_Key_Integrity(tableName, pkAttribute, newValue)
BEGIN
 SET query = "SELECT * FROM " + tableName + 
 " WHERE " + pkAttribute + " = " + newValue
 EXECUTE query
 IF resultCount > 0 THEN
 RETURN FALSE // Primary key violation
 ELSE
 RETURN TRUE // Value is unique, insert allowed
 END IF
END

// Usage in Insert Operation
PROCEDURE Insert_Employee(emp_id, name, salary)
BEGIN
 IF Check_Primary_Key_Integrity("Employee", "emp_id", emp_id) THEN
 INSERT INTO Employee VALUES (emp_id, name, salary)
 ELSE
 DISPLAY "Error: Duplicate Primary Key"
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm ensures entity integrity by checking that primary key values are unique before insertion, preventing duplicate records.

examples.title: ER Diagram Validation Rules
examples.description: Algorithm to validate ER model before implementation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Validate_ER_Model(ER_Diagram)
BEGIN
 // Check 1: Every strong entity has primary key
 FOR each entity E in ER_Diagram
 IF E.type = "strong" AND E.primaryKey = NULL THEN
 ADD error: "Strong entity " + E.name + " has no primary key"
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Check 2: Weak entity has owner relationship
 FOR each weak entity W in ER_Diagram
 IF W.ownerRelationship = NULL THEN
 ADD error: "Weak entity " + W.name + " has no owner"
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Check 3: All relationships have cardinality
 FOR each relationship R in ER_Diagram
 IF R.cardinality = NULL THEN
 ADD error: "Relationship " + R.name + " has no cardinality"
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Check 4: No redundant relationships
 FOR each pair (R1, R2) in relationships
 IF R1.entities = R2.entities AND R1.attributes subset R2.attributes THEN
 ADD warning: "Possible redundant relationship"
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN errorList
END
examples.explanation: This validation algorithm checks ER diagrams for common errors like missing primary keys, incomplete weak entity ownership, and missing cardinality specifications.
